_ai_hints.critical_instruction: This is a public whitewater river guidebook repository. Tag all content with hierarchical tags from this schema. Focus on rivers, rapids, difficulty, and navigation. DO NOT create tags related to personal vaults, work projects, or private systems.
_ai_hints.user_context: The repository maintainer is a technical whitewater kayaker creating GPS navigation tools for Garmin devices. Content is PUBLIC and for the paddling community.
_ai_hints.fragmentation_prevention: Do not create loose root tags. All tags must follow hierarchies: river/, type/, difficulty/, feature/, season/. Enforce consistency: use 'rapid' not 'rapids', 'campground' not 'camping'.
_ai_hints.public_repository: Everything here is openly published on GitHub. No personal information, no proprietary data, no private systems mentioned.

_system_context.description: Tag schema for KRB (Kern River Boater) - public whitewater guidebook with GPX navigation data
hierarchy_rules: type/... -> Content category (gpx, map, river_guide, tutorial, script)
river/... -> Specific rivers (kern, san_joaquin, rogue, etc.)
difficulty/... -> Whitewater class (class_i, class_ii, class_iii, class_iv, class_v)
feature/... -> River features (rapid, campground, access, parking, hazard, poi, milestone)
season/... -> Paddling season (spring_runoff, summer, fall, winter, year_round)
region/... -> Geographic region (california, southern_california, sierra_nevada, oregon)
flow/... -> Flow conditions (low, medium, high, flood)
status/... -> Data status (verified, needs_verification, outdated, deprecated)

_tagging_logic.note: AI INSTRUCTIONS: Use these heuristics to infer tags for whitewater guidebook content.
_tagging_logic.heuristic_rivers: Files in [River Name]/ folders get 'river/{river_name}'. Normalize: 'Kern' -> river/kern, 'San Joaquin' -> river/san_joaquin, 'Rogue' -> river/rogue. Use underscores for multi-word rivers.
_tagging_logic.heuristic_difficulty: Map rapid classifications to difficulty tags. Class II-III -> difficulty/class_ii_iii, Class IV -> difficulty/class_iv, Class V -> difficulty/class_v. If range spans multiple classes, use combined tag.
_tagging_logic.heuristic_features: Identify content type from filename or content. 'Rapids' -> feature/rapid, 'Campgrounds' -> feature/campground, 'Access' -> feature/access, 'Parking' -> feature/parking, 'Hazard' -> feature/hazard.
_tagging_logic.heuristic_file_type: GPX files -> type/gpx, JSON files -> type/data, Markdown with maps -> type/map, Tutorial content -> type/tutorial, Python scripts -> type/script.
_tagging_logic.heuristic_season: If content mentions flow/season: 'spring runoff' -> season/spring_runoff, 'summer flows' -> season/summer. Default for year-round navigable sections: season/year_round.
_tagging_logic.heuristic_region: Kern River -> region/california + region/sierra_nevada, Rogue River -> region/oregon, San Joaquin -> region/california + region/sierra_nevada.
_tagging_logic.heuristic_verification: New/untested data -> status/needs_verification, Field-verified -> status/verified, Old data needing updates -> status/outdated.
